TextENGNew: December 1, 1981
Shishibone City Welfare Department Document
changeReason: Old uses blank placeholder and wrong terms ('Prefecture', 'Juvenile Welfare Division'); new correctly renders 鹿骨市役所福祉部 as 'Shishibone City Welfare Department.'

TextENGNew: (Not for inspection. Not for reproduction.)
Case 31 (November 20)
Satoko Houjou
changeReason: Old adds placeholder '(_ years old)' not present in the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: Resident of Hinamizawa Village, Shishibone City
changeReason: Old translation uses a blank placeholder '___' and omits 'City'; the new correctly translates the full location from the JPN 鹿骨市雛見沢村在住.

TextENGNew: (1) How the Consultation Came About
An anonymous telephone consultation was received at the Okinomiya Life Consultation Center in Shishibone City.
changeReason: Old says 'SOS of child abuse' adding 'child abuse' not in the Japanese; new correctly translates the phone consultation details.

TextENGNew: (2) Situation
changeReason: Old translation adds 'Abuse' to the heading which is not in the JPN 状況 (Situation).

TextENGNew: A report alleging that a female child is experiencing difficulties in her home life with her legal guardians, the adoptive parents.
changeReason: Old says 'suffering physical abuse' but Japanese 生活上の問題 means 'problems in home life' -- a significantly different characterization.

TextENGNew: (3) Family Composition
Adoptive father, adoptive mother, older brother, the child in question
※ In June 1980, the child's parents died in an accident, and she was taken in by her paternal uncle (her father's younger brother).
(4) Response by the Okinomiya Life Consultation Center
changeReason: Old uses generic 'Child consultation center' while the JPN 興宮生活相談所 names the specific 'Okinomiya Life Consultation Center.'

TextENGNew: Upon receiving the anonymous telephone consultation, the center telephoned the child's school that same day and inquired into her circumstances.
The following day, the assigned caseworker visited the child's home and conducted an interview.
Both adoptive parents agreed to accept guidance from the consultation center.
The city's Family Guidance Division was notified.
changeReason: Old translates 生活指導部 as 'anti-abuse network' while the JPN specifies the city's 'Family Guidance Division,' a different type of organization.

TextENGNew: The case was classified as advisory guidance, with the local assigned caseworker thereafter conducting regular follow-up visits.
(The following hastily scrawled memo is stapled to the report.)
Related information available from Mr. Wada, the previous caseworker.
changeReason: Old abbreviates the name to 'Mr. W.' while the JPN clearly says 和田氏 (Mr. Wada); the full name and correct role 'previous caseworker' provide important document context.

TextENGNew: See document E2-3 No. 44 from 1977.
Chief Counselor Tanaka at the Shishibone City Education Consultation Center is well-informed on this matter; seek his advice.
changeReason: Old uses wrong name 'Chief Investigator F' while Japanese says 田中主査 (Chief Counselor Tanaka).
